CheckTitle: MySQL flexible server has high availability enabled
Description: **Azure MySQL Flexible Server** is evaluated for **high availability** mode. Zone-redundant or same-zone HA provisions a standby replica and provides automatic failover during planned and unplanned outages.
Risk: Without **high availability**, a server or zone failure causes **downtime** until manual recovery or redeployment. Applications experience extended unavailability and potential data loss for in-flight transactions during unplanned outages.

Code.Other:
1. In the Azure portal, open your Azure Database for MySQL flexible server
2. Under Settings, select High availability
3. Enable High availability and choose Zone redundant (or Same zone)
4. Save (high availability requires the General Purpose or Business Critical tier)

Recommendation.Text: Enable **high availability** (zone-redundant where supported, otherwise same-zone) on production MySQL Flexible Servers so a standby replica provides automatic failover. High availability requires the General Purpose or Business Critical tier; pair it with geo-redundant backup and periodic failover testing for full resilience.

Notes: High availability for Azure MySQL Flexible Server requires the General Purpose or Business Critical compute tier and is not available on the Burstable tier.
